brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (B) with precise appeal to Prigogine's far-from-equilibrium open systems and the relational-pattern-as-attractor mechanism; traces second-order effects (pattern constrains incorporation of new components, identity resides in structure not substrate) and explains the causal mechanism, not just the label; no explicit self-monitoring, bias-naming, or mid-analysis course-correction — the response proceeds linearly without interrogating its own framing; verifies by testing each rejected option against the concrete examples (e.g., checks whether each system produces its own components for autopoiesis), though does not apply a boundary-condition test to the winning option; acknowledges emergence is genuinely present in all three but insufficient, yet presents dissipative structure with full confidence and no stated conditions that would overturn the conclusion; systematically evaluates all three alternatives with specific disqualifying criteria for each (homeostasis = set-point regulation, not flux; autopoiesis = endogenous component production fails; emergence = too general); every conclusion is explicitly derived — the disqualification logic, the structural invariant, and the winning criterion are all traceable and reproducible from the response alone.
